reasoning: Fidelity composite critically low: 0.40 (3/3 dimensions evaluated). Document quality below minimum threshold for publication.

concerns.analysis: The output JSON contains a critical fabrication of contact information. On the first page/section, while the text content correctly displays 'annadockum@microsoft.com', the associated 'href' attribute in the JSON contains the email address 'mailto:ken.young@microsoft.com'. This name 'ken.young' does not appear anywhere in the source document, and substituting a different email address is a severe fidelity failure.

The output JSON explicitly states that 24 sections have been omitted (pages 4 through 27 of the source document). As a result, a vast majority of the source document's informational content is missing from the output conversion. Per the audit instructions, an entire section or page present in the source that is absent from the output is a critical defect.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Information on pages 4 through 27 (including Customer Needs, Microsoft Teams Premium features, Intelligent Collaboration details, and Rich Engagement features).

concerns.analysis: Several meaningful images from the source PDF are missing in the HTML output. Specifically, the summary circular diagram on slide 13 (which organizes all 'Rich Engagements' features) is absent. Furthermore, the QR code on slide 30, which is the primary content and functional purpose of the 'Follow-Up' slide, is missing entirely, leaving the final section of the document empty of its core information.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Source PDF, page 13 shows a circular diagram 'Microsoft Teams Premium The smart place to work' mapping various features; page 30 shows a QR code for 'NC DIT Innovation Summit Follow-Up'.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output for the slide 13 section shows only text followed by the next sub-header; the HTML output for the 'Follow-Up' section shows only the heading with no following image.
concerns.location: Sections 'Rich Engagements' (page 13) and 'NC DIT Innovation Summit Follow-Up' (page 30)

concerns.analysis: The email address on the first page has a malformed hyperlink. While the text correctly displays 'annadockum@microsoft.com', the 'href' attribute points to 'mailto:ken.young@microsoft.com'. This is a fabricated link target not found in the source text.
concerns.location: Section: 'Boosting Team Efficiency...', Page 1, element index 1
